summary: The implementation successfully covers most requirements. All 14 requirements have evidence of implementation across frontend and backend. However, there is one missing element: REQ-6 requires a 'Dining' category filter and products, but there is no dedicated Dining category page (only Living Room, Bedroom, and Office pages exist). Additionally, the backend includes a favorites feature that was not requested in the BuildRequest.

missing_requirements.requirement: Create a Shop page with category filters including 'Dining' - while the Shop page has a Dining filter button, there is no dedicated Dining category page like the other categories
missing_requirements.reason: BuildRequest specifies four category filters (Living Room, Bedroom, Office, Dining), but only three dedicated category pages were created. The Shop page includes a Dining filter, but users cannot navigate directly to a Dining category page from the navigation menu or have a dedicated Dining landing page with banner.

missing_requirements.requirement: Navigation dropdown should link to all category pages including Dining (implied by REQ-6's four categories)
missing_requirements.reason: The navigation dropdown under 'Shop' only includes Living Room, Bedroom, and Office links. There is no link to a Dining category page, which is inconsistent with REQ-6's specification of four categories.

hallucinated_features.description: Favorites/wishlist functionality allowing users to add and remove products from favorites
hallucinated_features.reason: The backend includes addToFavorites, removeFromFavorites, and getFavoriteProducts functions with associated data structures (favorites Map). This functionality was not requested in any requirement and is explicitly excluded by the non-goals which state no shopping cart or user account features beyond Internet Identity.
